name: Holo Code
summary: A sovereign, browser-first coding desktop: tabs, a Monaco editor, an integrated terminal, and a visual diff with accept/reject over the witnessed Holo Code engine. The brain is local verifiable inference (Holo Q), the tools work over the verified substrate, and every session is a receipt that proves itself genuine.

holo:dependsOn.holo:note: compose-by-reference: the GUI imports ../code/*.mjs at runtime; the engine is a hash-pinned dependency, never forked.

description.p: Holo Code is a graphical coding workspace panes you arrange, a file editor, an integrated terminal, a visual diff you accept or reject line by line. It is sovereign: nothing leaves your machine and nothing must be taken on trust.

It does not re-implement the agent it COMPOSES the witnessed Holo Code engine (apps/code) by reference: the same conscience-gated agent, the same content-addressed tools, the same Holo Q (QVAC) on-device brain. The Desktop adds only the GUI a 3-tab shell (Chat / Code / Cowork), a Monaco editor and an XTerm terminal reused from the OS workspace, and a diff view whose Accept/Reject resolves the engine's existing permission gate (Accept writes via the holo-files VFS; Reject is a no-op).

ul: Browser-first: the whole app is one sealed κ-object served from /ipfs/<cid>/, re-deriving byte-for-byte (Law L5)
Reuses the Holo Code engine by content-address no fork, no second brain
Monaco editor + XTerm terminal reused from the OS workspace, κ-verified before load
Visual diff with per-line Accept/Reject wired to the fail-closed conscience gate (ADR-033)
Execution-dependent features (run / build / test / terminal-exec / worktrees / schedule / ssh / cowork) are present but honestly gated to the native host never faked

releases.description: Holo Code a substrate-native, browser-first coding desktop, composed by reference over the witnessed Holo Code engine (apps/code). A 3-tab shell (Chat · Cowork · Code), a reused Monaco editor and XTerm terminal, and a visual diff with Accept/Reject wired to the fail-closed conscience gate. Chat runs on the local Q engine (Falcon-E-3B on WebGPU). Sealed as one re-derivable κ-object (Law L5); execution-dependent features are honestly gated to the native host.
